seoTitle: How to Fix an AGI Error in TurboTax
metaDescription: Resolve the AGI error in TurboTax with these troubleshooting steps so you can complete your e-file without delays or repeated rejections.
intro: An AGI error in TurboTax can hold up your e-filed return, and it commonly comes up for taxpayers with adjustments from a previous tax year. This guide breaks the fix into simple steps, from identifying the error to resolving it correctly. It's designed for both first-time filers and seasoned users who want to get past the issue quickly.

steps.heading: Identify the AGI error
steps.body: Confirm that the rejection or error message is specifically related to your Adjusted Gross Income before making changes.

steps.heading: Follow the step-by-step fix
steps.body: Correct the AGI figure in TurboTax, using the accurate number from your prior year's return.

steps.heading: Understand the implications of the error
steps.body: An unresolved AGI error can delay your tax return processing, so addressing it promptly helps avoid further complications.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for future filings
steps.body: Keep a copy of your prior year's AGI handy each filing season to avoid running into this error again.

faq.question: What causes an AGI error in TurboTax?
faq.answer: This error commonly arises when the AGI entered doesn't match IRS records, often due to adjustments made in a previous tax year.

faq.question: Where do I find my correct prior-year AGI?
faq.answer: Your prior year's AGI is listed on your previous tax return; if you don't have a copy, the IRS offers ways to retrieve this figure.

faq.question: Will fixing the AGI error delay my refund?
faq.answer: Resolving the error as soon as possible helps minimize any delay to your return processing and refund timeline.

faq.question: Is this error more common for certain types of filers?
faq.answer: Taxpayers with adjustments or amendments in a previous tax year are more likely to encounter this issue than those with straightforward prior returns.
